Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar May-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ASHWIN NANDA, TEL. NO. 1-415-974-1200, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. PAUL MINER OF THE BARRITT SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 1-949-553-0700 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Western Dental Services, Inc., Abdullah Munsaf, and Andre Azarinfar's motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend as to the first, third and fourth causes for breach of contract, wrongful termination and retaliation alleged against Mr. Munsaf and Dr. Azarinfar and denied as to the first and third causes of action for breach of contract and wrongful termination alleged against WDS. Plaintiff Ashwin Nanda has not alleged that Mr. Munsaf and Dr. Azarinfar were parties to Dr. Nanda's employment contract with WDS and it does not appear reasonably possible that Dr. Nanda could credibly make such an allegation and thus his first cause of action against them fails. Dr. Nanda's third and fourth causes of action fail against Mr. Munsaf and Dr. Azarinfar because supervisors are not liable for wrongful termination in violation of public policy or retaliation. (Reno v. Baird (1998) 18 Cal.4th 640, 664 (wrongful termination); Jones v. Lodge at Torrey Pines Partnership (2008) 42 Cal.4th 1158, 1173-1174 (retaliation).) On his first cause of action against WDS, Dr. Nanda is not required to attach a copy of the employment agreement or state its terms verbatim, but may instead plead the legal effect of the contract. (Miles v. Deutsche Bank National Trust Company (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 394, 401-402.) Paragraph 18 of the first amended complaint sufficiently pleads the legal effect of the alleged employment agreement between Dr. Nanda and WDS. Dr. Nanda's claim for wrongful termination against WDS is not time-barred. The statute of limitations on that claim was equitably tolled when Dr. Nanda filed his charge with the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission. Tolling satisfies the ends of "fundamental practicality and fairness." (McDonald v. Antelope Valley Community College Dist. (2008 45 Cal.4th 88, 99 (internal citation omitted).) Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ASHWIN NANDA, 415.994.1200, PLAINTIFF IN PROPRIA PERSONA.
DOUGLAS BARRITT, ESQ., 949.553.0700, FOR DEFENDANTS ABDULLAH MUNSAF, ANDRE AZARINFAR AND WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs ABDULLAH MUNSAF, ANDRE AZARINFAR and WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES, INC.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is granted, without leave to amend. A dismissal with prejudice is a final adjudication on the merits for the purposes of res judicata. (Boeken v. Philip Morris USA, Inc. (2010) 48 Cal.4th 788, 793.) While it is regrettable that Plaintiff inadvertently foreclosed his ability to proceed in state court on this primary right, he has done so. (See Samuels v. Northern Telecom, Inc. (1991) 942 F.2d 834.) This Court cannot offer the redress Plaintiff seeks unless the dismissal in federal court is set aside or reconsidered. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
